name: downstage
title: downstage.css + downstage.js — AI assistant guidelines
description: Conventions for building pages and apps with the downstage design system. Pair with README.md and docs/*.html for full detail.

sections.title: Stack and philosophy
sections.content:
- **Zero-build HTML/CSS/JS**: vanilla markup; no React/Vue requirement.
- **downstage.css** = layout, typography, components, tokens.
- **downstage.js** = optional enhancements (theme, navbar, tabs, accordion, lightbox, slider, media players, combobox, Kanban, data table, i18n, HTML editor, etc.).
- Prefer **semantic component classes** (e.g. `.btn.btn-primary`) and **CSS variables** over ad-hoc inline styles.
- **Accessibility**: preserve focus styles, labels, and ARIA where components use JS.

sections.title: Required and optional assets
sections.content:
- **Required for styling**: `downstage.css`.
- **Optional JS**: `downstage.js` with `defer` after CSS.
- **Icons**: `downstage-icons.svg` — reference icons with `<svg class="icon"><use href="downstage-icons.svg#icon-name" /></use></svg>` (sprite).
- **Fonts**: `fonts/` for Space Grotesk if you use bundled typography (see README).
- **Not for production apps**: `demo/` is for docs and demos only unless you intentionally reuse patterns.

sections.title: Document shell
sections.content:
- Set `<html lang="…" data-theme="light" | "dark" | "auto">`. `auto` follows `prefers-color-scheme` when wired with JS.
- Include `<meta charset>` and viewport meta.
- Typical main wrapper: `<main class="container">` (or other layout classes from README).
- Theme controls: buttons with `data-set-theme="light|dark|auto"` (handled by `Downstage.theme` when `downstage.js` is loaded).

sections.title: Design tokens
sections.content:
- Theme surfaces and type use **CSS variables** (`--bg`, `--text`, `--border`, `--brand-primary`, `--space-*`, `--radius`, etc.).
- **Override tokens** in a stylesheet loaded **after** `downstage.css`, usually under `:root` and `[data-theme="dark"]`.
- Do not hardcode hex colors for core UI if a token exists.

sections.title: downstage.js usage
sections.content:
- Global namespace: **`window.Downstage`**. Modules expose **`init()`**, run on `DOMContentLoaded`.
- After injecting new DOM that uses a component, call **`Downstage.<module>.init()`** again (or only the relevant module).
- Examples: `Downstage.theme.get()` / `set('dark')`; `Downstage.lightbox.open([{ src, alt }], index)`; `Downstage.kanban.mount(el, options)`; `Downstage.dataTable.mount(el, options)`; `Downstage.combobox.mount(el, options)`.
- Prefer **declarative attributes** documented in README (`data-tabs`, `data-accordion`, `data-slider`, `data-kanban-fetch-url`, …) when they cover the use case.

sections.title: Where to look in the repo
sections.content:
- **README.md**: quick start, tokens, components index, JS API tables.
- **docs/index.html**: hub to all topic pages.
- **docs/getting-started.html**, **docs/basic.html**, **docs/ui-components.html**, **docs/interactive.html**, **docs/media.html**, **docs/data.html**, **docs/templates.html**, **docs/apps.html**: deep examples and class names.
- **catalog-full.html**: single-page gallery; regenerate split docs with `python3 demo/split_catalog.py` when maintaining the project.

examples.title: Minimal page with theme and button
examples.code: Hello

antiPatterns: Shipping demo-only CSS (`demo/demo.css`, `demo/docs.css`) as if it were core — they style the documentation site, not your app.
Inlining large style blocks instead of extending CSS variables and existing utilities.
Initializing heavy JS widgets when the page only needs static layout — load `downstage.js` only when needed.
Breaking icon sprite paths — prefer serving the site over HTTP so `file://` restrictions do not block `<use href>` to external SVG.

cursorUsage.summary: This file is static JSON in the repo root. After deploy, it is available at the same path on your site (e.g. /downstage-ai-guidelines.json).
suggestions: Copy the `sections` and `examples` content into `.cursor/rules` (or `AGENTS.md`) for a project that uses downstage.
Point a custom MCP tool or script at the deployed URL to fetch and inject these guidelines into context.
Keep `schemaVersion` in sync when you change the shape of this file for automation.
